q: How does it calculate? Can I rely on this?
a: We use a 12% annualized return compounded monthly — the long-term benchmark for Indian equity mutual funds. This isn't a guarantee (no one can promise market returns), but it's the most widely used baseline for SIP planning across the industry. Think of your Freedom Age as a realistic north star, not an exact date.

q: Okay, I know my Freedom Age. What do I do now?
a: Start a SIP today. Open a free account on any SEBI-registered platform (Groww, Kuvera, Coin by Zerodha — all work), pick a flexi-cap or index fund, set up an auto-debit for your monthly amount, and forget about it. The single best thing you can do is start. Time in the market always beats timing the market.

q: Which fund should I invest in?
a: If you're unsure, start simple. A Nifty 50 index fund or a Nifty 500 index fund gives you broad market exposure at very low cost. No fund manager to worry about, no complicated strategy — just steady, long-term compounding. For most people, this is more than enough.

q: I can't afford to invest that much right now.
a: Then invest what you can — ₹500/month is a perfectly valid starting point. The magic isn't in the amount, it's in the consistency. As your income grows, bump up your SIP even by small increments. Every increase pulls your Freedom Age closer. The worst investment plan is the one you never start.

a: We'd love that. Drop us a note at iamnishantupadhyay@gmail.com — tell us who you are and what excites you. We're building simple, honest tools for personal finance in India, and we're always looking for curious people who want to build with us.
